title: rubus hirtus var. subhorridulus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. subhorridulus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry bush, is a type of raspberry plant native to the Himalayan region. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Rubus hirtus var. subhorridulus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
